mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust and expanding labor market, which directly translates into higher consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above a critical threshold signals persistent inflationary pressures, which typically prompts central bank tightening and erodes real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies robust consumer confidence and purchasing power, which directly boosts corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When RSAFS shows positive year-over-year growth and UNRATE is below 5%, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, sustained increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) raise borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing profit margins and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.25% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity market performance over short horizons.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE has risen by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts (HOUST) coupled with low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate a robust and affordable housing market, which stimulates construction, related industries, and consumer wealth effects, supporting positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST is above 1000k units and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, SPX 60-day returns are not consistently positive, failing a t-test for mean return > 0 (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, a significant acceleration in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust aggregate supply and demand, leading to higher corporate profits and positive equity market performance as investor confidence rises.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% annualized.
